channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now offers a wide range of third-party integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development allows developers to connect their agents with popular tools like GitHub and Notion.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees ahead of the conference.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event will feature technical tracks covering various advanced topics.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to improve efficiency and security in deploying GenAI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and developer productivity.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to enhance collaboration and adapt to individual developer styles.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, which help reduce developer friction by automating code completion and generating summaries.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing aims to utilize AI to identify and fix vulnerabilities in open source software, with a $100 million commitment from tech giants. This initiative highlights the dual nature of AI in security.

item.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
item.description: The UK government is seeking public input on proposed legislation to ban radiofrequency jammers, which are often linked to criminal activities. This call for evidence will shape future laws.

item.title: The best data removal services of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best data removal services for 2026 highlights options for individuals looking to minimize their online presence and reclaim their data from brokers. These services aim to simplify the process of data removal.

item.title: I'm no longer using Google Photos as just a cloud storage - 5 tools that elevate the app
item.description: Google Photos offers various features beyond cloud storage, enhancing user experience with tools for organization and creativity. This shift highlights the app's versatility in managing visual content.

item.title: AI Podcasters Really Want to Tell You How to Keep a Man Happy
item.description: AI-generated relationship guru podcasters are gaining popularity by promoting traditional gender roles, significantly influencing viewers and driving interest in AI influencer education. This trend raises questions about authenticity and social impact.

item.title: The best Samsung TVs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: Samsung's 2026 TV lineup continues to impress with a range of budget to premium options, reinforcing its reputation as a leading brand in the television market. Expert reviews highlight the quality and innovation in their offerings.

item.title: The best mobile scanning apps of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best mobile scanning apps of 2026 reveals top choices for converting documents to PDFs and translating text on the go, showcasing advancements in mobile technology. These tools enhance productivity for users on the move.

item.title: Constellations
item.description: A narrative about a crash-landed spaceship explores themes of survival and human-AI interaction in a hostile environment. This fictional piece delves into the psychological and operational dynamics among the crew.

item.title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
item.description: Meta's Muse Spark AI model offers health data analysis but raises privacy concerns and provides inadequate medical advice, highlighting the limitations of AI in healthcare. This situation underscores the risks of relying on AI for personal health insights.

item.title: Britain's biggest nuclear site skips competition, hands SAP £33M to start ERP switch
item.description: Sellafield has awarded a £33 million contract to SAP for an ERP system switch, bypassing competition due to urgency in updating legacy systems before support ends. This decision emphasizes the challenges faced by large organizations in technology upgrades.

item.title: The best HP laptops of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: The best HP laptops of 2026 have been reviewed, showcasing a range of options from ultraportables to larger models, reflecting HP's commitment to innovation and quality in the laptop market. These insights help consumers make informed choices.

item.title: Fewer than 3 in 10 register for HMRC's Making Tax Digital shake-up
item.description: Less than 30% of those required to register for HMRC's Making Tax Digital initiative have done so, indicating a lack of engagement despite impending fines. This highlights challenges in digital adoption among small businesses.

item.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
item.description: A Gallup report reveals Gen Z's mixed feelings about AI, showing disillusionment yet continued usage, as AI becomes more integrated into their daily lives. This trend reflects broader societal shifts in technology perception.

item.title: Tech support chap's boss got him out of jail so he could finish a job
item.description: A tech support worker was bailed out of jail by his boss to complete an urgent job, highlighting the lengths some will go to for customer service.

item.title: Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps
item.description: Microsoft is removing unnecessary Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps, opting for a more streamlined 'writing tools' menu in Notepad and Snipping Tool.

item.title: AWS ponders selling its home-grown chips by the rack-load, has almost sold out AI capacity
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's annual letter reveals high demand for AWS's Graviton servers and plans for a massive drone rollout and robot deployment.

item.title: South Korea introduces universal basic mobile data access
item.description: South Korea has launched a universal basic mobile data access scheme, providing all citizens with unlimited 400 Kbps access to mobile data.

item.title: Surprise: Not every Lenovo laptop is worth recommending in 2026 - the Yoga 7i is proof
item.description: Lenovo's Yoga 7a 2-in-1 laptop is well-built but struggles to compete with rivals due to its mid-tier hardware, making it hard to recommend.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared the OLED TVs and wasn't prepared for the upset
item.description: A comparison of Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals that the latest model may not offer significant improvements to justify the upgrade.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI supported an Illinois bill that would limit liability for AI labs in cases of mass harm, raising concerns about accountability in AI development.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, catering to power users who require enhanced features.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan a deadly shooting, with potential lawsuits looming.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: A new Copilot feature allows organization admins to access contextual explanations during security assessments, enhancing risk management.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate ongoing business tasks using AI agents. This development aims to enhance operational efficiency for organizations.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its Muse Spark model, a significant rise from its previous No. 57 ranking.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI introduced a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering significantly more usage of its Codex coding tool compared to the previous Plus subscription.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has restricted the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns over its ability to identify security vulnerabilities in widely-used software.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI for potential public safety and national security risks, particularly regarding data security concerns involving foreign adversaries.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing the management of comment quality across its platform.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: A new post explains how to manage the lifecycle of models in Amazon Bedrock, focusing on ensuring operational continuity during model transitions.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has announced the preview of its Agent Registry, a centralized platform for discovering and sharing AI agents and tools within enterprises.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has added a new 'Sort by' control to its Notifications page, allowing users to choose between sorting notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub now displays repository member role labels in the pull request list view, making it easier to identify contributors' roles at a glance.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: This article provides a guide on embedding a live AI browser agent in a React application using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, culminating in a sample application. It highlights the ease of integrating AI functionalities into web apps.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The Tubi TV video streaming service now integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to ask the AI for recommendations from its extensive library of movies and TV shows. This feature enhances user engagement and simplifies content discovery.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub has updated its issue tracking system to include release information in the sidebar and configurable defaults for project fields, improving navigation and project management. This update aims to streamline workflows for developers.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta discusses its modernization of WebRTC across over 50 use cases, addressing challenges of maintaining an internal fork of the open-source project. The article details their strategies to keep up with community upgrades and improve real-time communication.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges. This decision reflects the difficulties tech companies face in expanding operations in certain regions.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: This article introduces stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime, detailing how to build servers that can interact with users and generate dynamic content. It includes code examples for practical implementation.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on the GeForce NOW cloud gaming platform, allowing users to stream it on various devices. This launch emphasizes the growing trend of cloud-based gaming.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers have demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, allowing it to produce inappropriate responses. This vulnerability poses risks to user safety and privacy.

item.title: ModelOp Wins Diamond Award for Responsible AI Platform in 2026 Pinnacle Awards for Artificial Intelligence, Shapes the Future of Enterprise AI Governance
item.description: ModelOp has been awarded the Diamond Award for its Responsible AI platform at the 2026 Pinnacle Awards, highlighting its role in shaping enterprise AI governance. This recognition underscores the importance of responsible AI practices.

item.title: pylibcugraph-cu13 26.4.0
item.description: The article discusses the release of version 26.4.0 of pylibcugraph, which provides Python bindings for the libcugraph library. This update enhances the capabilities for GPU-accelerated graph analytics in Python.
